feed.title: SAP Community - SD (Sales and Distribution)
feed.subtitle: SD (Sales and Distribution) Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Price Group Condition ZA00 not working
entry.content: P80-658: group Pricing not working ZA00 condition

entry.title: Freight Condition ZHD0 not distributing to all lines on Invoice
entry.content:
We have a Freight Condition getting updated on delivery header directly . It is coming from interface.

When the invoice is created with reference to delivery, the entire freight value sits on the last time of the invoice. (Please note I am not referring to rounding value but entire freight value)

When the credit memo is created only for the last time, then seems like we are giving the entire freight value back to customer instead of the proportionate value, which is wrong and when credit memo is created for other lines, then there is no credit given to customer for freight. 

However, when we put  Freight condition type on the sales order, then we see that it distributes the freight across all lines. Same information is passed to delivery/invoice. 

And when the credit memo is created for a specific line item, then the proportionate freight is given to customer, which is right. 

I dont think so there is a config issue with condition type as it is same being used on order & delivery. 

So, can you please confirm if this is how SAP Standard works and if there is a way to distribute the freight on all the lines on the invoice if the freight is polulated on delivery directly.

entry.title: Minimize Custom condition types and account keys for REA1, RES2, REA5 etc. in settlement Management
entry.content:
Hello CCM Experts,

We have requirement to create custom condition types for different price promotions. There are around 26 conditions types in sales rebate we have created in place of RES1 so that each condition will be assigned to unique G/L Account.

Now for REA1, RES2, REA5 do we need to create 26 custom promotional conditions for each, as they are applicable for all promotional conditions types in accrual and reversal and we should also create custom account keys. This is making pricing procedure complex. 

Our aim is to assign each promotion condition to a specific G/L Account .

Could you please suggest alternativees to possibly reduce to one account keys for all these reversals.

Regards 

Kiran Kumar Dara 

Settlement Management  SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing  SD Sales

entry.title: Need to complete Sales order inVA02
entry.content:
Hello Team,
Need to complete sales orders which have incompleteness with Shipping point and net value as the feilds are greyed out in VA02 so we are not able to complete the orders.So many incomplete orders are there in V.02 since 2006 and it is affecting the performance of the report.
Could you please suggest further.

entry.title: error transaction INTRASALES- SAP
entry.content:
Business Scenario

T Code VEFU

During Intrastat reporting, we have scenarios where Credit Memo and Debit Memo billing documents need to be linked to the corresponding original invoice for reporting purposes. However, in certain cases, the material/article referenced in the Credit Memo or Debit Memo differs from the material/article in the original invoice.

As a result, the expected linkage between the adjustment document and the original invoice is not established in the Intrastat work file records.

Clarification Required

Could you please confirm:

  1. Does standard SAP ECC provide functionality to manually link a Credit Memo or Debit Memo to an original invoice for Intrastat reporting purposes when the material/article references are different? If yes, is there any standard transaction (e.g., VEFU or any other Foreign Trade / Intrastat transaction) that allows maintenance of the following Intrastat reference fields:
    • IREFBELEG (Reference Document)
    • IREFPOSIT (Reference Item)
    • IREFMONAT (Reference Month)
    • IREFEJAHR (Reference F

User facing this error while TO in SAP, packaging shipment 224558105

entry.title: Error in storage location determination using VA01 creating with reference
entry.content:
We have the wrong storage location determined in the return sales order 0888057809  

As per the configuration it should be picked 1002 it got picked with 1000

entry.title: user can not delete the inbound delivery, it shows
entry.content:
user can not delete the inbound delivery, it shows the confirmation cannot be deleted

Message no. ME635

entry.title: Can the Tax Code be Edited in a Debit Memo Created with Reference to a Sales Order
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a question regarding the standard behavior in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

When creating a Debit Memo with reference to a Sales Order, I noticed that the Tax Code is not editable.

I would like to confirm the following:

  1. Is there any standard configuration or business process that allows the Tax Code to be changed manually in the Debit Memo?
  2. If manual editing is not supported, what is the recommended SAP approach when a different tax code is required for the Debit Memo?

Any insights or references to SAP documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance !

Best regards, 

HY

entry.title: Storage Location Field Not Populating via Inbound ORDERS IDoc (ORDERS05) in QR1
entry.content:
We are currently experiencing an issue with sales order creation triggered by our external system. The inbound data is successfully received in SAP ECC via IDoc using message type ORDERS and basic type ORDERS05 in QR1.

Issue Description:
Although the storage location information is correctly populated within the incoming IDoc segments (as shown in the attached screenshot), the Storage Location (LGORT) field remains blank on the generated sales order line items.

 

To help us diagnose the root cause, could you please verify the following areas:

  • Segment Mapping: Confirm if the storage location value from the external system is mapped to the correct IDoc field E1EDP01 - LGORT

Could you please investigate this behavior and let us know why the storage location is failing to update?

entry.title: Clarification on Standard Processing of Multiple DELFOR IDocs
entry.content:
The sender system (Infineon) and the receiver system (Bosch) are both SAP systems.

Infineon sends Supplier Confirmations for the same Scheduling Agreement in two separate DELFOR IDocs (one containing Firm schedule lines and another containing Forecast schedule lines) in DELFOR D.97A/D.04A format.

We would like to understand the standard SAP behavior for processing these multiple inbound DELFOR IDocs. Specifically, how should SAP process Supplier Confirmations received in separate IDocs for the same Scheduling Agreement.

Expectation:

 

Is there any standard SAP functionality to merge or aggregate multiple inbound DELFOR IDocs into a single Supplier Confirmation before updating the Scheduling Agreement (EKES)? If yes, please provide the standard program, function module, class, or processing logic responsible for this scenario.

 

If no such standard functionality exists, please advise the recommended SAP standard approach for processing Supplier Confirmations received in multiple DELFOR IDocs for the same Scheduling Agreement.

entry.title: VA05 Report getting duplicate entries
entry.content:
Dear Team,

in VA05 Standard report ,We are getting  Duplicate entries.

due to this duplicate entries we are not getting proper report.

Thanks

entry.title: Condition contract (WCOCO) triggers error "Field KVGR1 of condition table A9DS is not supported"
entry.content: We have created new condition table 9DS in the (V/03) with the standard fields key combination COCO_NUM,KVGR1,KVGR2, KONDM,LAND1,REGIO for the condition type YS01 and for the condition contract type YR01, when we create the condition contract system triggers the error message "Field KVGR1 of condition table A9DS is not supported"

entry.title: Clarification on Standard Processing of Multiple DELFOR IDocs
entry.content:
The sender system (Infineon) and the receiver system (Bosch) are both SAP systems.

Infineon sends Supplier Confirmations for the same Scheduling Agreement in two separate DELFOR IDocs (one containing Firm schedule lines and another containing Forecast schedule lines) in DELFOR D.97A/D.04A format.

We would like to understand the standard SAP behavior for processing these multiple inbound DELFOR IDocs. Specifically, how should SAP process Supplier Confirmations received in separate IDocs for the same Scheduling Agreement.

entry.title: Industry Benchmarking to Implement SAP Ecosystem
entry.content:
One of the proven way to implementation SAP System in an enterprise and implement a Digital Transformation Strategy- follow the steps below:

  1. Focus on the current business process and clarify its flow.
  2.  Then Map the current business process with APQC process codes. (PCF- Global Framework)
  3. Follow OSB for Industry-Specific Process Benchmarking and develop Standards.
  4. Align the SAP Ecosystem as per Business Process Re-engineering

For more details, please comment your queries.

Follow the link to read more posts. https://www.linkedin.com/in/abhishek-khare-93a40237/

entry.title: S4 Advanced Returns Management - Logistical Follow-Up Actions
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

Happy Friday. I need some guidance regarding SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM).

In standard SAP, the Returns Overview for Customer Returns (transaction MSR_TRC_C) displays the standard follow-on material documents as Logistical Follow-Up Actions resulting from the inspection process (for example, blocked stock to unrestricted stock, blocked stock to returns stock, etc.).

In our scenario, however, the follow-on material documents are posted from decentralized EWM using non-standard processes (using Badi in EWM), such as:

  • Material-to-material transfer (movement types 309/343)
  • Immediate scrapping from blocked stock via PGI (movement type 555)

These postings create the corresponding material documents in S/4HANA.

The issue is that these material documents are not displayed as Logistical Follow-Up Actions in MSR_TRC_C. As a result, the processing status of the Logistical Follow-Up Actions remains yellow (Process Incomplete) instead of changing to green (Document Complete), even though the required material postings have been completed and the material documents properly link to the returns delivery/returns sales order.

We have thoroughly reviewed the standard ARM configuration but have not found any configuration option that supports this requirement.

Could you please advise whether there is a standard approach to achieve this functionality? Alternatively, are there any BAdIs, enhancement spots, implicit enhancements, or other extension points that could be used to populate the follow-on material documents and update the processing status accordingly?

Many thanks in advance for your assistance. If you need any additional information about our scenario, I would be happy to provide it.

 

Thanks,

MP

entry.title: Consignment Stock Return – Appropriate Customs Document for Portugal
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are currently working on a customer scenario and would like to understand how others handle a similar situation.

Our customer receives consignment stock from one of their suppliers. The goods are accompanied by a pro forma invoice with a value of EUR 0.00, which is used only for transportation purposes.

However, in Portugal, Customs generally only accepts pro forma invoices in specific situations (for example, when shipping samples). This has raised the following question:

What is the most appropriate document to accompany the return of consignment stock while complying with Portuguese Customs requirements?

If anyone has dealt with a similar scenario, I would appreciate your insights, especially regarding:

  • Which document do you use to accompany the return shipment?
  • Are there any best practices or specific Portuguese Customs requirements to consider?
  • How have you implemented this process in SAP?

Thank you in advance for sharing your experience and recommendations.

entry.title: Duplicate entries in Report VA05
entry.content:
VA05Document Date        25.08.2024      To   27.08.2024All orders

Sales Organization :  00D1
Distribution Channel   : O2
Division            : 02
Sales office      :   D111
Sales Group     :     FDUyou will get Report, and download to Spreadsheet, and filter with Material  " Y1040T3  "you will get same line item number duplicate entry.

entry.title: Handling KBETR/KOMV-KBETR Overflow for Large IDR Pricing Values in SAP SD
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement in SAP SD Pricing where large pricing values in IDR currency are causing an overflow due to the standard length limitation of the pricing rate field (KONP-KBETR / KOMV-KBETR).

Current Scenario for Indonesia

  • Condition records are created through a custom program (not manually via VK11).
  • The source system sends very large IDR amounts.
  • During condition record creation, the value is assigned to the pricing condition and eventually exceeds the technical limit of KBETR, resulting in an overflow/runtime error.
  • Business requires the original IDR amount to be displayed on sales orders, invoices, reporting, and accounting documents.

We have checked multiple options so far:

  1. Divide the IDR value by a factor (100) before condition record creation and multiply it back during pricing/billing.

    • Concern: precision loss and rounding differences.
    • Additional concern: determining which factor was used during invoice generation.

  2. Investigated KPEIN (Pricing Unit), but in our case the problem is not a very small unit price. The issue is that the actual condition rate itself exceeds the KBETR technical limit.

Has anyone faced a similar issue with high-value IDR pricing conditions ? Any recommendations, SAP Notes, or implementation experiences would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: VA01 vertical scrollbar visible only after horizontal scrolling
entry.content:
Hi,

I am using an SAP test system based on Global Bike as part of my studies. I noticed a usability issue in transaction VA01.

When the SAP GUI window is maximized, the right-side vertical scrollbar is visible and the bottom horizontal scrollbar is not needed. However, when the window is reduced, the bottom horizontal scrollbar appears and the vertical scrollbars move to the far right side.

I have also seen a case in another S/4HANA system where these scrollbars were displayed even when the SAP GUI window was maximized. In that case, the user had to move the bottom horizontal scrollbar to the right in order to access the vertical scrollbar. After doing this, the left part of the screen was cut off and fields/tabs such as Order Reason were no longer visible.

This is not convenient when creating sales orders where the Order Reason field needs to be used, because the user has to scroll horizontally just to access the vertical scrollbar, and then loses visibility of the left side of the screen.

I checked a similar VA01 layout in SE51. The main screen was SAPMV45A / 4001,  and the lower area was defined as SUBSCREEN_BODY.

My questions are:

  1. From a technical point of view, can this behaviour be changed by adjusting the dynpro/subscreen layout in SE51/SE80?
  2. Is the scrollbar position controlled by the width/position of the lower subscreen area, for example SUBSCREEN_BODY?
  3. Or is the scrollbar position fully controlled by SAP GUI / standard SAP screen behaviour?
  4. Why does the screen not adjust to the available SAP GUI window size in the same way as in the old SAP system?

I understand that changing SAPMV45A  would be a standard modification, but I would like to understand what technically controls the position of this vertical scrollbar and why the screen does not resize in the same way as in the legacy SAP system.

If this is not the correct board, please advise where this SAP GUI / dynpro layout question should be posted.

 

Thank you.
